R^ ^ ? ^ ^ RT F ' Rl RT^ RR ^ 3 3 7 R^ RsT'tT ^f ^] R^ ^^RTRRT RT^T3TT Ri RRTJ fRFTTR RTFR RT RRT ^ R R S ^ RRTRT ^TTRRI I (3 } ~*it£ ^ R^R&T m WT^q ^R ^> TTT^q m{\^ FR R( RTR STR ^fRR^ RRt' R^ IRTR ^ ^ R # | | 10. rRTRRR, F^TRT ^RTRT 5RT 34lcbfU|cb frfRR "5R ^ T ^TTRT.-( ] ) RTS RiT ST^T^ 3TR RTRRT R ^ t R RRqi c^RTT^ RT F R ^ R H ^ RFT^t R, RRTTRRTR. 3T^R^ R* RRfR RFT'TF3!!! -- 2 of 5 -- qRcT ^T TT^m : 3TOMTW . 3 (2) ^\i^] 3Tfc7«J ^TT ^ ^ P T , ^ # q m ^ R ^ ^^nSTRfl feTfecT ^T tf ^RT SRI 3N^ "^ ^T r*TFT ^R ^£rr[ (*!) ^ , ST^TSI ^ft TR, W ^TRT 3 3Tf*W ^ cfti cfft ^ 7 ^Ff ^ i f t ; (•Ef) ^ , ^Nrq m^R ^ im ^', S N ^ -^i ^r 1%%^ ^ ^r ^ M f ^JT "qr^H ^ "3 s m ^ i ; ^r 1 1 . 3 H f V l V ^ r ^ , - - ^ «£ ^eqjff afk 3T^f W * t f ^ " ^ T ^ R f T O 3 ^ TTcT? sfo *ffif, feff <£ T?TRf 3TR ^ t £ ^ 31N^+{ WTTI [ ^ . ^' 5/27/2006-^t.^eT-5] MINIVTRY OF COMPANY AFFAIRS NOTIFICATION New Delhi, the 27th November, 2006 G.S.R. 734(E).—In exercise of the powers conferred by clauses (e) and (f) of Sub-section (2) of Section 38A of, read with Section 29C and Sub-section (1) of Section 29D of, the Cost and Works Accountants Act, 1959 (23 of 1959), the Central Government hereby makes the following rules namely:— 1. Shorttitle and commencement.—(1) These Rules may be called the Cost and works AccounLanLs Procedures of Meetings of Quality Review Board, and Terms and Conditions of Service and allowances of the Chairperson and members of the Board Rules, 2006. (2) They shall come into force on the dale of Ihcir publication in [he Official Gazette. 2. Definitions.—(1) In these Rules, unless the context otherwise requires, (a) "Act" means the Cost and works Accountants Act, 1949; (b) "'Board" means the Quality Review Board formed under subjection (1) of Section 29A of the Act; (c) " Council" means the Council constituted under Section 9 of the Act; (d) "Institute" mean the Institute of Cost and Works Accountants of India set up under the Act. (2) Words and expressions used but nol defined in these Rules and defined in the Act shall have the same meaning as assigned to Lhem in the Act. 3. Procedure for Meetings of the Board.— (J) All meetings of the Board shall, ordinarily, be held at the headquarters of the Institute. (2) The date and time of any meeting shal] he fixed by [lie Chairperson : Provided that a notice of not less than 15 days before the scheduled date of every such meeting shall be given by the Chairperson or any person so authorised by. him to the other members of the Board : Provided further that the Chairperson, for reasons to be recorded in writing, may call for a meeting at a shorter notice ; Provided also that no! more than six months shall elapse between any two meetings. (3) The quorum for transaction of any business at a meeting of the Board shall be of three members, of which at least one must he nominated by the Central Government under Sub-section (3) of'Section 29A of the Act. (4) The Chairperson shall chair all the meetings of the Board ; Provided that in the absence of Chairperson, the members shall elect any of the members present on the day of the meeting to chair the meeting. I'mII—T3P5 3(i)] -- 3 of 5 -- 4 Till! UAZHITI- OF INDIA ; EXTRAORDINARY |P-XR:!1 S K . 3 ( I ) | (r>) All questions which come up before any meeting of the Hoard shall be decided by a majority of the members present and voting, and in the event of an equality of voles, the Chairperson or in his absence, the member presiding, shall have, a second or easling vo;e. 4. Transaction of Business.—The business of the Hoard snail ordinarily be transacted at a meeting of [he Hoard. 5. Agenda for the Board meeting.—(1) The agenda for a meeting of the Board shall be decided by ihe Chairperson. (2) Any mailer referred to ii by Ihe Central Government or the Council shall be included in die agenda for the meeting at the Hoard within lime limns specified, if any, in such a reference. (3) The Chairperson may, in appropriate eases, circulate the agenda note and related papers on any issue among members of the Board for resolution by circulation. Provided thai if three members of the Hoard require that any question be decided at a meeting, ihe Chairperson shall withdiaw the papers from circulation and have the question determined at a meeting of the Hoard. (4) A decision taken by the circulation of the papers shall be communicated to all Ihe members and shall be noted ai the next meeting of the Board. 6. Procedures lo be followed by the Board.— In the discharge of us functions, the Hoard may : (a) on its own or through any specialized arrangement sei up under the Institute, evaluate and review ihe quality oi work and services provided by ihe members of the Institute in such manner as it may decide; (h) lay down the procedure or evaluation criteria lo evaluate various services being provided by the members of the Institute and to select, in such manner and form as il may decide, the individuals and firms rendering such services for review; (e) call for information from Ihe Institute, the Council or its Committees, members, clients o!"members or other persons or organisations in such form and manner as it may decide, and may also give a hearing to them; (d) invite experts lo provide expert/technical advice or opinion or anahsis or anv matter or issue which the Hoard may feci relevant for the purpose of assessing the quality of work and services offered by the members of the Institute; (e) make recommendations to Ihe Council to guide ihe members of the Institute to improve their professional competence and qualifications, quality of work and services offered and adherence to various statutory and other regulatory requirements and other matters related thereto. 7. Assistance to the Board.—The Institute shall set up a specialised technical unit to— (a) provide secretarial assistance, as required, to the Board in the discharge of its functions: (b) lo assist the Board in carrying out review of quality of services provided by ihe Members. 8. Terms and conditions of service of the Chairperson and Members of the Board.—(1) The tenure of the Chairperson and Membei> of the Board shall be four years. (2) The Chairperson and other members of the Hoard shall be pan-lime members. (3) No Chairperson or member of the Hoard shall be removed or withdrawn except as provided under Rule 10. 9. Allowances.— (1) The Chairperson and other Members shall be entitled to travelling and daily allowances as admissible to them in their official capacity, and incase, the . member is not a Government Servant, he will be entitled to the iraveliing allowance and daily allowance as admissible to an officer holding a post carrying a scale of pay of a Joint Secretary lo the Government of India for aUcndingmeeiings of the Board at the headquarters of the Institute if their place ol residence is different from ihe headquarters of the Institute. (2) The Chairperson and other Members of the Board whose place of residence is the same as the venue of the meeting of [he Board shall be provided local conveyance or alKvance for the local journeys lo be performed in connection with [he meetings of the Board. (_.-) The Chairperson and other Members of the Board, while on tour, shall be entitled lo travelling and daily allowances at (he above rates. 10. Resignation, Removal and filling up of casual vacancy.— (1) The Chairperson and each Member of the Hoard shall cease to remain the Chairperson or Member, as the case mav be, in ease of his resignation or removal. (2) The Chairperson or ;i Member of the Board may resign his office by a no!ice in writing under his hand addressed to the Central Government which shall be effective from ihe date of resignation. (3 (The Central Government may remove a person from the post of Chairperson or Member, if -- 4 of 5 -- [•*M1I'—J^j'iilL v{Us\^]7JW^ : 5T?PTRW D (a) he has become physically or mentally incapable of acting as the Chairperson or a Member, as the case may be; (b) he has not attended three consecutive meetings of ihe Board, without leave of absence; (c) he, being the Chairperson, has not called a meeting of the Board lor more than six months; (d) he, in the opinion of the Central Government, is unable to discharge his function or perform his duties; or (e) he has been held guilty by any civil or criminal court for an offence which is punishable with imprisonment for a term exceeding six months. (4) A casual vacancy in the Board shall be filled by the Central Government, from out of the category in which such vacancy occurs. 11. Residuary provision.— Matters relatin lo the terms and conditions of services and allowances of the Chairper- son and other members of the Board, Ihe place of meetings and the procedure lo be adopted in meetings of the Board, with respect to which no express provisions has been made in these rules shall be referred in each case to the Central Govern- ment for its decision and the decision of the Central Government thereon shall be binding on the Board, the Chairperson and oilier members.
